changelog:
v2.0.2.3
- Fix: Megaplay downloads still failing after all retries ("ffmpeg exited with code 234: ... Output file does not contain any stream") — megaplay.buzz's HLS master playlist exposes each bitrate rendition as its own ffmpeg "program", which broke ffmpeg's automatic stream selection and left it mapping zero streams. ffmpeg is now told explicitly which video/audio stream to grab (-map 0:v:0 -map 0:a:0) instead of relying on automatic selection.

v2.0.2.0
- Fix: Anikoto episode titles were never populated (dropped during the v2.0.1.0 rewrite) — now filled from the API's per-episode title, falling back to "Episode N" since Anikoto rarely provides one.
- Fix: Megaplay downloads failing after all retries ("ffmpeg exited with code N") — megaplay.buzz's CDN checks the Referer header on the actual video/segment requests, not just the API calls used to resolve them, and ffmpeg was sending none. IStreamExtractor now has an optional RequiredReferer that gets passed to ffmpeg via -headers.
- Improvement: ffmpeg failures now include the last few lines of ffmpeg's own stderr in the error message (e.g. an HTTP 403/404 from the CDN), instead of just the bare exit code — makes future download failures much faster to diagnose.

v2.0.1.0
- Fix: Anikoto "Failed to load series"/missing thumbnails — GetSeriesInfoAsync was calling a /api/anime/:slug endpoint that belongs to a different, unrelated community project and 404s on the actual deployed wrapper API. Series metadata (title/poster/genres) is now carried through from search/browse results instead of a second API call.
- Fix: Anikoto search/browse/episode parsing corrected against live API responses (search results' "slug" field includes a "/ep-N" suffix that must be stripped; episodes' "server_ids" is a single opaque string, not an array).
- Fix: Anikoto stream resolution — the API's /api/stream endpoint returns a megaplay.buzz embed page, not a direct video file. Added a MegaplayExtractor (decodes the real HLS URL from the embed page) instead of treating the response as a directly downloadable stream.
- Change: Only Anikoto's "HD-*" server is offered as a download option for now — VidPlay/Vidstream/VidCloud resolve through other embed hosts this plugin doesn't have a verified extractor for yet.
- Fix: Episode filenames/duplicate-detection for flat-episode-list sites (Anikoto) now use the real episode number instead of falling back to a slug+timestamp name and mis-detecting every episode as "already downloaded".

v2.0.0.0
- Renamed to AniBridge Downloader to reflect multi-site support (previously AniWorld Downloader).
- Feature: Pluggable site architecture — adding a new streaming site is now a single service class registered in PluginServiceRegistrator, with no controller/config changes required.
- Feature: Added Anikoto (anikoto.net) support, English Sub and Dub, via a community-documented third-party API.
- Feature: Added Anime Nexus (anime.nexus) support as an experimental, disabled-by-default adapter (unverified — anime.nexus has no public API and blocks automated research tools).
- Change: English Sub/Dub only across every source. All German language options (German Dub / German Sub) have been removed, including from AniWorld and s.to.
- Change: Language keys simplified from numeric (1/2/3, per-site meaning) to canonical "sub"/"dub" everywhere — config download paths, API responses, and history records.
- Change: Replaced all emoji in the UI with lucide.dev icons.
- Change: Config UI reorganized into one section per site; "Only allow German" toggle and numeric language-fallback-order option removed in favor of a single Sub/Dub fallback checkbox.
- Removed: The /Flag endpoint and per-language flag SVGs — languages are now shown as icons (captions = Sub, mic = Dub) rendered client-side.
- Removed: Dead GenericStreamingService reference that no longer compiled.

Previous versions were released as "AniWorld Downloader":

v1.6.1.0
- Added language fallback. Thanks to Alexius25 on GitHub
- Removed remaining dead HiAnime code
- Removed: FlareSolverr integration. s.to has moved its provider redirector behind a Cloudflare Turnstile widget that activates per-IP for flagged egress addresses. FlareSolverr cannot solve embedded Turnstile widgets that require user interaction, so the integration was not actually helping affected users. If your server's IP is gated, route the plugin's outbound traffic through a clean proxy via the existing Proxy Server setting (SOCKS5 / HTTP supported).

v1.6.0.0
- Feature: Movie download support for s.to and aniworld.to (#17, thanks @Alexius25)
- Feature: Force option to re-download episodes already marked as downloaded
- Feature: SOCKS5 / HTTP proxy support for outbound traffic to streaming sites
- Feature: Manual "Rebuild download history" scheduled task to resync the database with files on disk
- Fix: Movie detection and downloading reliability
- Fix: Removed legacy scheduled task that was a no-op

v1.5.3.0
- Feature: Configurable s.to base URL in plugin settings — use an alternative domain (e.g. serienstream.to) if s.to is blocked by your ISP
- Fix: ffmpeg not found on Windows servers with custom Jellyfin install locations — removed hardcoded fallback paths in favor of Jellyfin's configured ffmpeg path

v1.5.1.0
- Feature: Maintenance mode to block users from adding episodes to queue
- Feature: Priority queueing for downloads
- Feature: Display user and language for queue elements
- Feature: Better formatted statistics in history view

v1.5.0.0
- Feature: HiAnime.to support for English dubbed anime (later removed)
- Feature: Sidebar access for non-admin users via File Transformation plugin
- Feature: Separated download paths per language

v1.4.0.1
- Change default AniWorld provider to Vidmoly (faster downloads) with VOE as fallback
- Set default s.to provider to VOE

v1.4.0.0
- Feature: s.to series download support

v1.3.2.0
- Feature: Improved download queue display (downloads tab)

v1.3.1.0
- Wider Jellyfin compatibility (10.9.0+, down from 10.11.0)
- Jellyfin plugin repository support for easy install and updates

v1.3.0.0
- Removed automatic update check (updates tracked via Jellyfin plugin repo)

v1.2.0.0
- Feature: Repository link button (GitHub icon) in header

v1.1.0.0
- Security: SSRF protection, ffmpeg argument injection prevention, XSS hardening
- Feature: Browse tab with Popular and New Releases sections

v1.0.0.0
- Feature: Download All Seasons

v0.1.0.0 - Initial release
description: Search, browse, and download anime and series from multiple streaming sites (AniWorld, s.to, Anikoto, Anime Nexus) directly within Jellyfin. English Sub/Dub only — no German. Adding a new site is a single pluggable adapter class.
name: AniBridge Downloader
overview: Download anime and series from multiple streaming sites directly within Jellyfin (English Sub/Dub only)
